summary: Two Important References emails arrived overnight: one from Muhammad Usman (MOM) on site operations and safety, one from Collins Amponsah re-circulating a 6-month-old Maaden complaint on HOD coverage. Together they signal two distinct risk threads — a survey production gap requiring urgent recovery, and a leadership governance gap that hasn't been resolved since December 2025.

sources.title: MOM — Site Operations & Safety Review (June 18, 2026)
keyPoints: MTD survey confirmed at 460,076 BCM — 6% behind monthly target. With ~12 production days elapsed, recovery requires sustained above-plan daily rates for the final 12 days of June.

The MOM's "6% behind target" framing understates the truck-vs-survey gap: trucks show 533,293 BCM MTD, meaning the survey-truck reconciliation gap is ~73,217 BCM (−13.7%). The 6% figure references survey vs the monthly production target, not the internal truck count.
5 million man-hours without a Lost Time Injury (5M LTI) achieved. Significant milestone for the JRAR contract.
MOM confirmed Sunday June 21 as the EX-123 return-to-service target. This represents a 2-day slip from the original plan. Eight consecutive days at 0% (stick cylinder leak, Date In 11-Jun-26).

actions.action: Confirm EX-123 stage-readiness for Sunday June 21 return — check stick cylinder repair completion status with workshop
Monitor daily survey rate: need ~22,400+ BCM/day survey through June 30 to close the 6% gap
Stage-6 cleanup crew and equipment assigned for June 19
Prepare June KPI supporting data for HO submission

sources.title: FW — Operational Continuity: HOD Coverage and Shift Communication

actions.action: Establish a formal HOD coverage roster — designating named deputies before any HOD takes leave, with Collins and superintendent notified in advance
Confirm current HOD coverage for this week (June 19 onwards) is in place — no simultaneous absences
Raise HOD coverage governance in next operations meeting as a standing agenda item
Acknowledge Collins Amponsah's re-circulation directive — confirm receipt and planned action

crossCutting:
### Survey recovery trajectory
The MOM email gives the official survey MTD position (460,076 BCM, 6% behind target). This aligns with but differs from the internal truck-count MTD (533,293 BCM). The ~73,000 BCM gap between trucks and survey is the persistent reconciliation issue driven by excavators loading outside survey calculation boundaries (EX-253 and EX-262 have been the recurring drivers). Closing this gap requires either getting excavators within boundary or negotiating survey methodology adjustments.

### EX-123 return dependency
The Sunday June 21 EX-123 return is now confirmed at MOM level, meaning it is a committed deliverable. Any further slip will require proactive communication upward. The stick cylinder repair must be verified complete before Sunday's shift start.

### Leadership visibility
Collins' re-circulation email signals that both safety and operational continuity issues are being escalated through the JRAR chain. Peter Anthony sits at the intersection of both threads — HOD coverage is a leadership governance issue within his remit, and the survey recovery is a production management issue. Both have been flagged to management level.

---
